claim.statement: Use autofocus sparingly. Auto-focusing an input can drop screen-reader and cognitively impaired users into a field without the surrounding context and can trigger an unexpected change of context. Only autofocus when a field is unambiguously the primary task and it benefits all users.

limitations: Single-purpose pages (e.g. a search-only or login screen) are reasonable exceptions

do: Reserve autofocus for single-purpose screens where the field is the clear primary task
Ensure preceding instructions are still encountered before focus lands
On mobile, weigh the disruption of an immediately opening keyboard
avoid: Autofocusing fields buried below important context or instructions
Autofocusing inside modals that also shift screen-reader context unexpectedly
